title: oxalis coralleoides
intro: Oxalis coralleoides, also known as the coral oxalis or coral wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's leaves and flowers contain oxalic acid, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Consuming the leaves or flowers may provide health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving digestion, and lowering blood pressure, but caution is advised due to the high oxalic acid content. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be used as a natural food additive or ingredient in salads, but it is essential to use it in moderation and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
